Partitive-genitive superlative 'best among sages'.

p.p.p. bhakta 'devoted/attached to the master' — locative object of devotion.

sarva (sarvanāma adjective) + noun in the same case: 'all living beings'.

Agentive kṛt -in (vardhin, √vṛdh) with object kula: 'increasing the family'.
Shouts of/proclaiming victory; genitive, jaya = content of the śabda.
Measure-compound 'mountain-sized'; -mātra determinative, genitive of measure.
Honorific śrī + Lord in apposition, same case (samānādhikaraṇa).

Indra's command/word; genitive.

Gods and demons (a battle 'of gods and demons'); coordinate.

head vāhin is a kṛt agent noun from vah; śoṇita is its accusative object → upapada.
adjective mahat and noun bhūmi share case, simple attribution → karmadharaya.
ppp+noun describes an external referent (the bow-wielder), not the bow itself → bahuvrihi.
head jit is a kṛt agent noun from ji; prameha is its accusative object → upapada, matches codebook example.
bandha (action noun) construed with genitive complement nāda, naming a yogic technique → tat-sasthi.
adjective mahat agrees in case with kratu → karmadharaya.
demonstrative etad functions as a case-agreeing determiner of artha → karmadharaya.
pronominal adjective sarva agrees in case with vajraudana → karmadharaya.
reflexive adjective sva agrees in case with rājya → karmadharaya.
antara 'other' functions as a case-agreeing adjective on viṣaya → karmadharaya.
superlative sattama takes the genitive-plural sense of the compared group → tat-sasthi.
sutra cites two coordinate particles namas and puras jointly → dvandva.
past participle uddhata takes instrumental cause bala → tat-trtiya.
passive participle ukta takes instrumental agent bhavat → tat-trtiya.
action noun sevana (from sev) takes genitive object kāma per karmaṇi-ṣaṣṭhī rule → tat-sasthi.
describes the two armies/combatants by a possessed attribute (arisen anger), an external referent → bahuvrihi.
participle viśiṣṭa agrees in case with guṇa, simple attribution → karmadharaya.
proper name nandana in apposition with generic noun vana, same case → karmadharaya.
head madhya takes genitive complement śyena, 'the midst of' relation → tat-sasthi.
collective numeral sahasra (head) takes genitive complement varṣa; member1 itself is not the numeral, so not dvigu → tat-sasthi.
collective numeral śata (head) takes genitive complement yojana, same pattern as varṣa-sahasra → tat-sasthi.
past participle saṃtapta takes instrumental cause śoka → tat-trtiya.
coordinate pairing of being and non-being → dvandva.
two coordinate qualities listed together describing a substance (Āyurvedic guṇa-dvandva) → dvandva.
passive participle prokta takes instrumental agent veda → tat-trtiya.
action noun vacana (from vac) takes genitive object adhikṣepa → tat-sasthi.
generic noun pada names/stands in apposition with ajala, same case → karmadharaya.
head āvaha is a kṛt agent adjective from ā-vah; vijaya is its accusative object → upapada.
member1 ṣaṣ is a numeral quantifying head manu as a defined set → dvigu.
superlative śreṣṭha takes the genitive-plural sense of the compared group kuru → tat-sasthi.
anīka (troop) takes genitive complement gaja → tat-sasthi.
pronoun mad in genitive sense (mama) modifies bhakta → tat-sasthi.
kṛt suffix -ja 'born from' takes ablative source sirā → tat-pancami.
abstract-noun suffix tva forms a bhāva noun, not a case-inflected relation covered by the seven vibhakti types or other subtypes → tat-other.
kvātha (decoction) takes genitive complement bhallātaka → tat-sasthi.
describes a person possessing dear fame, an external referent → bahuvrihi.
adjective pūrva agrees in case with janman → karmadharaya.
type-name ṣoḍaśin in apposition with generic noun pātra, same case → karmadharaya.
collective numeral traya (head) takes genitive complement dina, which is not itself the numeral → tat-sasthi.
'X-ādi' list-marker compound denotes an external referent (the class of items), classic bahuvrihi pattern → bahuvrihi.
Head vāja is dictionary-masculine but inflected feminine here (agreeing with the aids/ūtī it modifies), the classic exocentric-gender signature of bahuvrihi.
Abstract '-tva' head denotes the condition/state OF member1, a genitive relation.
Superlative śreṣṭha compounds with a genitive-sense comparison set, standard ṣaṣṭhī-tatpuruṣa.
Deverbal 'stha' (from sthā, stative) expresses a locative relation to member1, unlike the accusative-governing 'gata'.
'hutabhāga' is the standard dictionary gloss for a possessive epithet of the gods (recipients of the offered portion).
Adjective+noun in the same case, straightforward apposition.
Adjective 'mahā' directly qualifies 'mantra' in the same case.
Duties belong to/pertain to the four-āśrama system, genitive relation.
'-rogin' is a state/affliction adjective taking a locative complement, like the śauṇḍa/nipuṇa class.
kula denotes a collection OF member1, genitive relation.
Instrumental agent of the past participle sṛṣṭa.
kṛt agent noun '-da' (< dā, ends in -a) takes member1 as its object, canonical upapada.
kṛt agent adjective '-jña' (deverbal, ends in -a) takes member1 as its object.
Infinitive-stem+kāmyā compound expresses purpose, the tumun-elided caturthi (dative-sense) pattern.
Instrumental means of the causative participle rodhita.
Body-part noun + participle is the canonical possessive/exocentric bahuvrihi pattern.
'vātsalya' (fondness) takes a locative object of affection, like sneha/bhakti patterns.
Deverbal participle '-bhakta' (< bhaj) takes member1 as its object/goal of devotion.
Matches the codebook's own 'grāma-gata' example exactly — accusative goal of motion with deverbal 'gata'.
The compound characterizes 'āhava' (battle) exocentrically ('[battle] in which the sun vanished'), not the sun itself.
kṛt agent noun '-vid' takes member1 as its known object.
śrī is dictionary-feminine but inflected masculine here (agreeing with the man Prātardani), the exocentric-gender bahuvrihi signature.
'ṛṣabha' belongs to Pāṇini's vyāghrādi group of eminence-simile words forming karmadhāraya (2.1.56), not a genitive superlative.
Causative kṛt agent '-prayojaka' takes the reciprocal member1 as its object.
'anta' is dictionary-masculine but inflected neuter here (agreeing with rūpam), the exocentric-gender bahuvrihi signature.

Numeral+body-part describing the (feminine) goddess vocatively, though bhuja is dictionary-masculine — possessive bahuvrihi, not dvigu, since it attributes a quality to a person rather than naming a counted set.
The army belongs to the Yādavas, genitive relation.
Simile-apposition compound describing the horses, both members effectively same-case.
Locative site of the heating (Rasaśāstra jaṭharayantra apparatus).
Matches the codebook's own caturthi trigger-word 'artha' exactly.
Locative site where the vīṇā is made to hang.
'yoga' (application/process) pertains to the named technique, genitive relation; glossed in-text as synonymous with saṃmardana-yoga.
'kalpa' (procedure/rule) pertains to the caru-offering, genitive relation.
Same '-ādi/-ādika' exocentric 'and others' pattern as cpd_token_id 425799.
'vitti' (perception) has antarātman as its object of cognition, genitive relation.
Member1 is a free absolutive (gerund) merely sandhi-fused to a following genitive noun, not a genuine nominal compound; insufficient structure to assign a Mahābhāṣya class.
'anta' is dictionary-masculine but inflected neuter here, the same exocentric-gender bahuvrihi signature as cpd_token_id 4748445.
Quantifier adjective 'sarva' directly qualifies prāṇin in the same case.
'mūtra' (urine) belongs to/is produced by the goat, genitive relation.
vardhinī is a deverbal -in agent noun (causative √vṛdh); kula is its object — she who increases the family line.
quotative apposition: the cry/word 'jaya' — both members denote the same referent (the shout itself).
-mātra size-simile compound (upamāna karmadharaya), parallel to the codebook's megha-śyāma type.
honorific apposition: 'he who is glorious and blessed', both epithets predicated of the same referent in the same case.
taddhita -tva 'state of X' is genitive per Pāṇini's tasya bhāvaḥ rule.
nāśinī is a deverbal -in agent noun; deha is its object (body-destroying, epithet of death/old age).
adjective + noun apposition in the same case: 'worldly/common examiners'.
locative relation — attached AT the neck (sacred thread hanging around the neck).
two distinct co-equal ritual acts (muttered recitation and fire-oblation) joined by 'and'.
the door OF Saubha (the flying city/vehicle) — genitive relation.
same -in agent pattern as deha-nāśinī: fever is the object of destroying (a fever-remedy plant).
the word OF Indra — genitive; the compound's external ablative case is a distractor.
bhuj is a kṛt agent noun (√bhuj); pattra (leaf) is its object — 'leaf-eater'.
anveṣin is a deverbal -in agent noun; sukha is the object sought.
adjective + noun, same case — a family marked/characterized by epilepsy.
duties/rites performed FOR the gods — dative of purpose, analogous to yūpa-dāru.
bhettṛ is a kṛt agent noun (√bhid+tṛc); Rādhā (the target) is its object.
vartin is a deverbal -in noun; vaśa (control) is the argument of the verbal action — 'one who abides in [another's] control'.
coordinate pair 'gods and demons' — the battle of both parties together.
vocative epithet addressing a woman by an external possessed feature — exocentric, denotes her, not the face.
adjective + noun, same case — friendship characterized as base/low.
eka functions adjectivally ('a single/the same'), not forming a numeral-aggregate set — contrast dvigu.
sons OF a [good] family — genitive.
the drug/herb FOR the [alchemical] puṭana process — dative of purpose.
describes the vehicle as possessing eight wheels — exocentric possessive.
the tip OF kusha-grass — genitive.
the root OF the puṣkara plant — genitive.
the center OF Amarāvatī — genitive.
a forest OF palm trees — genitive.
obscure Vedic epithet, 'swift-flowing/swift-gifted', describing an external referent (path/course) rather than the stream itself; low confidence.
the heat OF the sun — genitive.
the passing/emission OF waste — genitive (bowel movement).

vigraha: devān heḍayati tat / devān prati heḍanam
rationale: heḍana is a verbal-action noun (√heḍ); deva is its object — offense committed against the gods.
coordinate pair — the wife and the sacrificer, named together as ritual participants.
simile-compound (upamāna karmadharaya) — resembling a lotus in lustre.
describes a person via a possessed feature (his face) — exocentric referent.
stha is a kṛt-deverbal locational noun; saudha (palace-terrace) is its locus argument.
daughter OF a [good] family — genitive.
pierced/perforated BY/WITH a hundred [holes] — instrumental means (a ladle with a hundred perforations).
taddhita -tva, 'the state of being a piśāca' — genitive per Pāṇini's tasya bhāvaḥ rule.
Warrior-epithet 'one whose hand is (of) iron' — external referent dominates (anyapadārtha), so bahuvrīhi, not tatpuruṣa.
